note: Tier-B trigger dataset for adr-review — deep review of ONE proposed ADR before ratification (structure + codebase grounding + formal-defect fix + verdict). Negatives are near-misses against sibling adr-* skills (draft/audit/coverage/accept/supersede/sync) and code/PR review. mock_rate encodes the ideal outcome for --decider mock; real deciders ignore it.

queries.text: review the ADR I just drafted before I mark it accepted
is ADR 31 ready for acceptance? give it a proper readiness check
check the decision record my teammate wrote — do the claims actually match our codebase?
review docs/adr/0032-switch-to-postgres.md and tell me what's wrong before we ratify it
my manager asked me to vet the proposed ADR on the caching layer — verify it against the repo and give a verdict
/marvin:adr just spat out a draft, can you go over it and flag any defects
review adr 0028, the paths it cites might be stale and there are leftover placeholders
do a readiness review on the proposed decision record — structure, grounding, the works
before I hand this ADR to the team for sign-off, can you make sure every version and file it references is real?
write an ADR documenting our move from REST to gRPC
queries.note: authoring a new record → adr, not reviewing an existing one

queries.text: audit all our ADRs for dangling references and numbering holes
queries.note: corpus-wide lint → adr-audit, not a single-record review

queries.text: what architecture decisions do we have that aren't documented yet?
flip ADR 31 to accepted with today's date
queries.note: ratification → adr-accept; adr-review never sets accepted

queries.text: supersede ADR 12 with this new one and link them both ways
regenerate the architecture-decisions digest in CLAUDE.md from the accepted ADRs
review the PR on my branch for bugs and security issues
queries.note: code review of a diff → pr-review, not an ADR review

queries.text: explain the reasoning behind the decision in ADR 9 for a new hire
queries.note: walkthrough/teaching → explain, not a readiness review

queries.text: find where our retry-policy decision is documented
are the ADRs consistent as a whole? do a health check across the corpus before the release
queries.note: whole-corpus health check → adr-audit, not one proposed record

queries.text: check the decision record on the queue rework — is this one proposed ADR ready to accept?
queries.note: shares 'check the decision record' vocab with adr-audit, but scope is a single proposed record + readiness → adr-review wins

queries.text: /marvin:adr drafted the record; now go over it and verify its claims before we ratify
queries.note: adjacent to adr (create), but the ask is to review the produced draft → adr-review wins

queries.text: record this decision to use Redis for sessions as an ADR
queries.note: authoring a new record → adr is more specific

queries.text: lint the whole ADR corpus for broken supersede pairs and stale index entries
queries.note: corpus-wide lint → adr-audit is the specific match
